name: pytest-mastery
description: Python testing with pytest using uv package manager. Use when: (1) Running Python tests, (2) Writing test files or test functions, (3) Setting up fixtures, (4) Parametrizing tests, (5) Generating coverage reports, (6) Testing FastAPI applications, (7) Debugging test failures, (8) Configuring pytest options. Triggers: "run tests", "write tests", "test coverage", "pytest", "unit test", "integration test", "test FastAPI".
capabilities: Run pytest tests with uv package manager
Create and use pytest fixtures with different scopes
Generate coverage reports in multiple formats
Test FastAPI applications with TestClient and httpx
Debug test failures with pytest options
author: AbdullahMalik17
securitySummary: This is a legitimate testing documentation skill. Static analyzer flagged 110 issues, but all are false positives: markdown code fences were mistaken for shell backticks, and keywords in test examples triggered crypto/network patterns. The actual code is a pytest runner that executes tests locally with hardcoded arguments.
valueStatement: Writing tests for Python applications can be confusing without guidance on fixtures, parametrization, and coverage tools. This skill provides ready-to-use pytest patterns including FastAPI testing, fixtures, and coverage reporting.
userTitle: Write and run Python tests with pytest
limitations: Does not generate test code from specifications
Does not integrate with CI/CD pipelines

useCases.title: Add tests to Python projects
useCases.description: Set up pytest with fixtures and parametrization for Python libraries and applications

useCases.description: Write integration tests for FastAPI endpoints using TestClient and async testing patterns

useCases.description: Run tests with coverage analysis and generate HTML or XML reports for CI/CD pipelines

promptTemplates.prompt: Run all pytest tests with uv package manager

promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pytest fixture for database connection with proper teardown

promptTemplates.title: Parametrize tests
promptTemplates.scenario: Running same test with multiple inputs
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a parametrized test that checks multiple input combinations

promptTemplates.title: Coverage report
promptTemplates.prompt: Run pytest with coverage and generate an HTML coverage report

bestPractices: Use fixtures for reusable test setup instead of repeating initialization code
Run coverage reports regularly to identify untested code paths
Use parametrization to test multiple input cases with a single test function
antiPatterns: Avoid hardcoding test data directly in test functions
Do not skip fixture scopes without considering resource usage
Avoid mixing unit and integration tests in the same test suite without markers

faq.question: Which Python versions support pytest?
faq.answer: pytest supports Python 3.8+ and works with uv package manager on all major platforms.

faq.question: What is the maximum test count per run?
faq.answer: pytest runs all discovered tests. For large projects, use markers or parallel execution with pytest-xdist.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with CI/CD tools?
faq.answer: Commands can be run in any CI/CD pipeline that supports Python and uv. Use --tb=short for cleaner logs.

faq.question: Is test data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: All tests run locally. Coverage reports are generated locally and never leave the machine.

faq.question: Why are my tests not being discovered?
faq.answer: Ensure files match test_*.py or *_test.py pattern. Functions must start with test_ and classes with Test*.

faq.question: How does this compare to unittest?
faq.answer: pytest offers simpler syntax, powerful fixtures, parametrization, and better reporting compared to Python's built-in unittest.
